codeReview.summary: The fix correctly addresses the fire-and-forget goroutine error loss issue in blacklists.go. All 4 goroutines (2 in addBlacklist, 1 in removeBlacklist, 1 in buttonHandler) now use error_handling.RecoverFromPanic() per CLAUDE.md patterns. DB functions AddBlacklist, RemoveBlacklist, and RemoveAllBlacklist were updated to return errors, and all goroutines now check and log these errors using structured logging with log.Fields.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed CLAUDE.md goroutine patterns correctly: defer error_handling.RecoverFromPanic(context, func) at the start of each goroutine, followed by defer wg.Done() when applicable. This is the documented pattern.
Worker correctly updated DB functions to return errors instead of silently ignoring them, matching the CLAUDE.md pattern of 'Never ignore DB errors with _'.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: alita/db/blacklists_db.go:11 - func AddBlacklist(chatId int64, trigger string) error, returns err from CreateRecord and nil on success

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker properly structured error logging in goroutines with log.Fields containing context (chatId, word, error) for debugging.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: alita/modules/blacklists.go:133-136 - log.WithFields(log.Fields{chatId, word, error}).Error("Failed to add blacklist")

sharedStateObservations.observation: Cache invalidation in DB functions occurs after successful operations (not on error path), consistent with CLAUDE.md 'Cache must be invalidated on writes' pattern.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: alita/db/blacklists_db.go:28 - deleteCache(CacheKey(...)) called after CreateRecord succeeds and before returning nil

sharedStateObservations.observation: Test file was properly updated to handle new error return signatures, ensuring backward compatibility for tests while validating the new error handling.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: alita/db/blacklists_db_test.go:18 - if err := AddBlacklist(chatID, "badword"); err != nil { t.Fatalf(...) }

summary: The fix for critical blacklists goroutine errors (VAL-CRIT-002) is complete and correct. All fire-and-forget goroutines now properly handle errors and have panic recovery. The implementation follows CLAUDE.md patterns for error handling, goroutine safety, and cache invalidation. No blocking issues found.
